  3 ## [1/42] [Light] Project Light Attack and Defence Leads
  4 Source: gchat | Group: space/AAQAsFyLso4/rKtZEQsyOFI | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-08T00:50:45.697000+00:00 | People: Michael Bui, Alvin Choo, Hengky | Labels: api-governance, vendor-standards, documentation-strategy, stakeholder-coord, tech-leadership
  5 In a direct conversation on April 8, 2026, Michael Bui sought clarification on the 'front office' initiative to publish API documentation using OpenAPI specs. Alvin Choo directed Michael to contact Hengky, who manages the publishing page. Michael expressed interest in contributing his team's API documentation to this centralized repository for consistency. This interaction is highly relevant as it pertains directly to Michael's Engineering Governance mandate of standardizing backend architecture and API documentation practices across vendor integrations (specifically CoMall). It also connects to his ongoing efforts with RMN, Display Ads, and PLP ad architectures where standardized documentation is critical. While the previous session established Confluence as the primary tracking tool for long-term specs, this discussion explores a complementary channel for open-source style publishing, requiring Michael's technical judgment on format conversion (Confluence to OpenAPI) and potential coordination with external stakeholders.
  6 
  7 ## [2/42] [Leads] (Ecom/Omni) Digital Product Development
  8 Source: gchat | Group: space/AAQAN8mDauE/txicES320JM | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-08T01:41:13.767000+00:00 | Work Items: DPD-898, BCRS | People: Michael Bui, Sneha Parab, Daryl Ng, Wai Ching, Rajesh | Labels: resource-allocation, deployment-critical, ticket-tracking, team-capacity, b2b-delivery
  9 Michael Bui is directly engaged in a critical resource allocation discussion for the BCRS (1HD) PRD deployment. Following Daryl Ng's request to utilize Wai Ching after his current ticket completion, Sneha Parab clarified team capacity constraints and confirmed Wai Ching would start work on DPD-898 today (April 8th). The conversation highlights an urgent dependency on this specific deployment for Rajesh (PM), who requires it by the end of the week to support B2B timelines. While Michael initially sought updates on the ticket status and owner, he received confirmation that Wai Ching is assigned. This interaction underscores Michael's dual mandate: ensuring delivery leadership meets tight deadlines while monitoring engineering governance regarding resource availability and SLO adherence against weekly deployment targets.
 10 
 11 ## [3/42] [Light] Project Light Attack and Defence Leads
 12 Source: gchat | Group: space/AAQAsFyLso4/28ecuxsQb4I | Relevance: 8/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-08T02:58:07.199000+00:00 | Work Items: fpg-pandora-preprod, fpg-optimus-preprod | People: Alvin Choo, Daryl Ng, Michael Bui | Labels: architecture-governance, team-restructure, environment-decision, catalog-enrichment, legacy-systems
 13 In a Google Chat discussion within the 'Project Light Attack and Defence Leads' space, Michael Bui provided critical architectural context regarding two pre-production environments: fpg-pandora-preprod and fpg-optimus-preprod. When Alvin Choo and Daryl Ng questioned the distinction between these services, Michael explained they originated from the now-defunct Search and Browse teams within the Grocery Tribe and have become confusing following a restructure to the Staff Excellence Tribe (DPD). To resolve the ambiguity, Michael shared visual evidence of both environments. The conversation concluded with Alvin Choo seeking clarification on whether maintaining product enrichment for the catalog requires keeping both projects active, indicating an ongoing decision point that aligns with Michael's role in engineering governance and architecture standards.
 14
